risks.description: The spec mandates BroadcastChannel + storage-event fallback and idempotent application of mutation events, but it does not define a deterministic conflict resolution policy (e.g., last-write-wins vs. causal ordering vs. per-field merges). Without this, concurrent edits across tabs can lead to lost updates or flip-flopping state.

DB migration and position rebalancing are called out, but the spec lacks concrete thresholds and algorithms (e.g., min allowed delta between positions before rebalance, exact resequencing step value, migration rollback instrumentation). Implementing safe, tested migrations requires these specifics to avoid data loss.

IndexedDB availability and quota failure handling are required, but browser-specific failure modes (Safari private mode limits, Chrome quota behaviors, file system-backed IndexedDB differences) are complex. The spec requires test coverage, but does not prescribe deterministic fallback/time-to-notify strategies; these are critical for reliability.

Optional encryption is specified at a high level, but important parameters are unspecified (KDF choice and iterations, salt & IV handling, key derivation storage of metadata, clear UX for key change/loss). These omissions can cause incompatible exports/imports or insecure defaults if implemented by an agent without cryptography expertise.

Undo semantics and optimistic rollback are specified, but persistence semantics across reloads and multi-tab scenarios are not fully defined (e.g., should soft-deletes survive a page reload during undo window, how undo tokens are synchronized across tabs). Lack of specifics can cause inconsistent user experience.

Import behavior references validation and migrations but does not define ID collision / deduplication policy, merge vs replace semantics, or preview/merge UI. These gaps can lead to accidental data loss or confusing import flows.

risks.category: Service worker update UX and safety
risks.description: SW is optional and guidelines are given, but the exact caching strategy (cache-first vs network-first for app shell and for assets), update notification to user, and migration of cached assets are not prescriptive. Mistakes here cause stale UI or confusing update behavior.

The test matrix and coverage targets are appropriate, but running cross-browser E2E (Playwright) on PRs and maintaining 5k-item performance tests will require CI resources and orchestration not fully detailed in the spec (parallelization, test flakiness mitigation, costs).

recommendations: Define a deterministic cross-tab conflict resolution strategy (for example: per-entity last-write-wins using op timestamps + opId tie-breaker, with an option to surface merge conflicts to the user). Document message ordering guarantees and how to handle out-of-order or delayed messages.

Specify concrete rebalance/migration parameters: minimum allowed delta between positions before rebalance, rebalancing step (e.g., 1000), and a safe resequence algorithm with sample pseudocode. Add unit tests explicitly exercising precision edge-cases.

Provide exact IndexedDB fallback and notification flows: detect failure modes (open error, quota write failures), define immediate user-facing messages, and a deterministic fallback policy (in-memory -> localStorage only after explicit consent). Include Playwright test cases that simulate these failures.

Lock down encryption parameters in the spec: choose PBKDF2 iterations (or Argon2/scrypt), salt length, key length, AES-GCM IV handling, and a metadata format for encrypted exports. Add cryptographic unit tests and a clear UX warning about irrecoverable loss.

Clarify undo semantics across reloads and tabs: specify whether soft-deletes persist to storage until undo window expires, how undo tokens are stored and synchronized, and what happens if a tab closes during the undo window. Add integration tests for these scenarios.

Define import conflict resolution policies (merge by id, dedupe by checksum, or allow user to choose replace/merge) and implement a preview/dry-run mode in the UI. Add zod schemas and deterministic ID collision tests.

Provide a canonical BroadcastChannel message handling sequence with timestamps, sourceId ignore logic, and an idempotency mechanism (opId tracking). Include tests that send out-of-order messages and assert correct final state.

Provide example CI GitHub Actions workflow(s) in the repo (lint → unit tests → build → bundle-size check → security scan → E2E matrix → deploy). Include caching and parallelization hints to manage runtime and cost.

Add explicit SW caching strategy and update UX to the spec (e.g., cache-first for static assets, network-first for HTML, show banner prompting users to refresh when new SW is available). Include tests to ensure no stale assets are served on deploy.

Add a small, opinionated UI/UX appendix for critical flows: exact fields and validation messages for create/edit task/project, undo toast design and default timeout (e.g., 8s), confirmation dialog copy for destructive actions, and import preview UX. This reduces ambiguity for a coding agent producing UI.
Provide sample migration test fixtures and a migration runner used in unit tests so an implementation can be validated from version N→N+1 automatically.
Document exact bundle-size threshold in CI config and list of allowed third-party dependencies with upper-version bounds to make bundle budget enforceable.
